channel.title: Tech on MinWeb
channel.description: Recent content in Tech on MinWeb

item.title: The Onion Buys Infowars: Turning a Conspiracy Hub Into Satire
item.description: Discover how The Onion is turning the notorious conspiracy site Infowars into a platform for satire in an audacious effort to dismantle misinformation today.

item.title: The AI Paradox: Why Your Productivity Isn't Improving
item.description: Despite massive investments in AI, productivity remains stagnant; discover why the modern AI paradox is mirroring history and what it means for your workflow.

item.title: Palantir CEO Pushes for Mandatory Universal Service
item.description: Discover why Palantir CEO Alex Karp is advocating for mandatory national service and how new government policies are moving toward automatic draft registration.

item.title: SpaceX Bought 18% of Tesla Cybertrucks in Q4 2025
item.description: New data reveals that SpaceX accounted for 18% of all Tesla Cybertruck registrations in Q4 2025, raising questions about internal demand and corporate synergy.

item.title: Allbirds Pivots to AI: Stock Soars 300% After GPU Shift
item.description: Allbirds has stunned the market by ditching sustainable footwear to become a GPU-as-a-Service provider, resulting in a dramatic 300% surge in its stock price.

item.title: Rockstar Games Data Breach: What Actually Happened
item.description: Get the facts on the Rockstar Games data breach and learn how a supply-chain attack on a third-party service compromised data without affecting game...

item.title: Why Gen Z Is Sabotaging Corporate AI Rollouts
item.description: Gen Z employees aren't just sabotaging AI out of fear; they are reacting to a fundamental disconnect between corporate pressure and ineffective implementation.

item.title: John Deere Settles Right-to-Repair Case for $99 Million
item.description: John Deere has agreed to a $99 million settlement, marking a major victory for the right-to-repair movement and granting farmers access to essential repairs.

item.title: Why Honda’s President Fears the Chinese EV Revolution
item.description: Discover why Honda’s President believes legacy automakers are losing the race to Chinese EV manufacturers in this deep dive into the industry's innovation gap.

item.title: Why Modern Headlights Are Blinding Drivers: It’s Not Just You
item.description: Are you tired of being blinded by oncoming traffic? Discover the real reasons behind the global headlight glare crisis and why modern cars feel so bright.

item.title: Iran Threatens to Destroy $30B Stargate AI Data Center
item.description: The $30 billion Stargate AI data center is now a geopolitical target after Iranian threats, raising critical questions about the future of global tech security.

item.title: The $5 Billion Analog Rebellion: Why Gen Z Is Going Offline
item.description: Discover why Gen Z is driving a $5 billion analog rebellion by ditching smart tech for reliable, simple hardware to escape the exhaustion of constant...

item.title: Why Gabe Newell Stepped Back From Making Valve Games
item.description: Discover why Gabe Newell intentionally stepped back from Valve’s creative process to prevent his influence as CEO from stifling his team's innovation.

item.title: Microsoft Pivots Back to Native Apps to Fix Windows Bloat
item.description: Microsoft is shifting back to native Windows applications to replace resource-heavy web wrappers, aiming to reduce system bloat and improve performance.

item.title: AI Insurance Denials: When Algorithms Decide Your Care
item.description: Learn how insurance companies are using AI algorithms to rapidly deny medical claims and what this shift means for your access to necessary healthcare coverage.

item.title: Why Your Survival Shouldn’t Be Tied to Your Paycheck
item.description: Discover why relying on employer-provided benefits creates dangerous instability and explore the historical context of tying your survival to your paycheck.

item.title: The Hidden Cost of Job-Linked Benefits: A Tragic Case Study
item.description: The tragic story of a terminally ill employee losing his benefits reveals the dangerous instability of tying essential health and life insurance to employment.

item.title: The Dodgers' Digital-Only Policy Excludes Loyal Seniors
item.description: The Dodgers' mandatory digital ticketing policy is alienating long-time senior fans, raising concerns about accessibility and the real cost of innovation.

item.title: Sony Increases PS5 and Pro Prices: What You Need to Know
item.description: Sony has announced significant price hikes across its entire PlayStation 5 lineup; find out what these increases mean for gamers and why costs are rising.

item.title: Why OpenAI Is Shutting Down Sora: The Economics of Failure
item.description: OpenAI shuttered Sora due to unsustainable costs and poor revenue, proving that even revolutionary AI models must face the harsh reality of economic viability.

item.title: US Pays TotalEnergies $1B to Scrap Offshore Wind Projects
item.description: The U.S. government is paying TotalEnergies nearly $1 billion to scrap offshore wind projects, marking a major shift in national energy and climate policy.

item.title: Leonid Radvinsky Dies at 43: What’s Next for OnlyFans?
item.description: Following the death of Leonid Radvinsky at 43, explore how the future of OnlyFans faces a critical transition and what it means for the platform's legacy.

item.title: Windows 11 Update: Taskbar Freedom and No Forced Updates
item.description: Microsoft is shifting its Windows 11 strategy with a new Quality Roadmap that prioritizes user control, system performance, and flexible update scheduling.

item.title: The FBI Is Buying Your Location Data—And Won’t Stop
item.description: The FBI is bypassing warrant requirements by purchasing your mobile location data from commercial brokers, turning mass surveillance into a legal loophole.

item.title: The FBI’s Data Broker Loophole: Tracking You Without Warrants
item.description: Discover how the FBI exploits a legal loophole by purchasing your location data from private brokers, effectively bypassing the Fourth Amendment and warrants.
